stories.title: Biggest winners -- and question marks -- from Roun...
stories.summary: Our ESPN draft insiders break down twists, turns and trades of Tuesday's first round of NBA draft

stories.title: Wizards opt for BYU's Dybantsa with No. 1 pick
stories.summary: The Wizards ushered in a new era by drafting AJ Dybantsa with the first pick, selecting him over Kansas' Darryn Peterson to help turn the franchise around.

stories.title: Celtics' Stevens: Brown remains 'big part of us'
stories.summary: Amid reported trade talks for Jaylen Brown, Celtics president of basketball operations Brad Stevens said Tuesday night that the star forward remains "valued" by the organization.

stories.title: Johnson reunites with May, goes No. 9 to Mavs
stories.summary: Michigan forward Morez Johnson Jr. was taken with the ninth pick by the Mavs and his former coach at Michigan, Dusty May, and was one of three Wolverines to go in the lottery along with Yaxel Lendeborg and Aday Mara.

stories.title: Ghana: 'VAR went for coffee' to aid 'lucky' Englan...
stories.summary: Ghana boss Carlos Queiroz joked that the video assistant referee must have gone for a coffee after saying England escaped a penalty and red card in Tuesday's goalless World Cup stalemate.
